title: When Being 'Helpful' Wasn't
summary: The time Alex's helpfulness crossed a line and damaged a friendship

story.hook: I need to tell you about the time I tried to 'fix' someone's entire life. <break time="150ms"/>Uninvited.
story.setup: My friend was struggling. Missed deadlines. Disorganized. Stressed. And I thought—I can help with this. I'm GOOD at this.
story.conflict: So I made her a color-coded calendar. A task management system. Follow-up reminders. The whole thing. <break time="150ms"/>I sent it to her. <break time="200ms"/>Proud of myself.
story.peak_moment: She didn't respond for two days. <break time="200ms"/>When she finally texted, she said: <break time="150ms"/>'I didn't ask for this. I told you I was struggling because I wanted support. Not a system. You made me feel like I'm a problem to be solved.'
story.resolution: She was right. <break time="200ms"/>I'd been so focused on what I could DO that I never asked what she NEEDED. <break time="150ms"/>Sometimes people want presence, not productivity. <break time="200ms"/>We're okay now. But I'll never forget how my 'help' felt like judgment.
story.callback: Now I ask first. <break time="100ms"/>Always. <break time="150ms"/>'Do you want me to help, or just listen?'

teaching_moment: Helping without consent isn't help. It's control disguised as care.

voice_notes.pacing: Slow at the peak moment. Let the weight of it land.
voice_notes.key_line: You made me feel like a problem to be solved
